title: DDS Net Accuracy Rate by State, FY2007–FY2022 (latest-vintage alias)
description: Latest-vintage pointer to the SSA federal quality-assurance net accuracy rate for state Disability Determination Services (DDS), by state and federal fiscal year, with a NATIONAL roll-up row per year. Net accuracy = share of sampled initial determinations with no documented deficiency affecting the decision. This `-LATEST` alias always reflects the newest published vintage; it currently equals the FY2007–FY2022 vintage.

columns.name: STATE
columns.description: State/territory name in uppercase (includes DIST OF COLUMBIA, PUERTO RICO, and a NATIONAL roll-up row per year).

columns.name: NET ACCURACY RATE
columns.description: Net accuracy rate as a percent string, e.g. '98.10%'.

columns.name: SAMPLING VARIABILITY (SV) PERCENT
columns.description: Sampling variability. Usually a decimal percent (e.g. 1.15); occasionally a confidence-bound text like 'UL* 99.8 - LL** 98.1' or blank when not published.

columns.name: COMMENTS
columns.description: Optional note, e.g. 'SV manually computed'. Usually empty.

geographic_coverage: All U.S. states + DC + Puerto Rico, with NATIONAL roll-up
notes: Authoritative: official SSA federal QA publication. This is the '-LATEST' latest-vintage pointer; it currently equals the dated sibling dds-net-accuracy-by-state-2007-2022.csv (same FY2007–FY2022 coverage, same 848 data rows, byte-for-byte values identical). It is NOT byte-identical to that sibling: the dated 2007-2022 vintage interleaves blank separator lines between data rows whereas this LATEST file is clean (no blank lines); after dropping blank lines the data is identical. NET ACCURACY RATE is a percent string ('98.10%') — strip '%' to compute. SV is mixed-type: parse a leading float where present, otherwise treat as text. Source provenance: dds-net-accuracy-by-state-2007-LATEST.csv.source.txt (latest FY: 2022).
